question_text: Hi IT, something really strange happened after the security changes that were announced recently, and now multiple departments are stuck. First: in Sales, several people can receive emails fine, but anything they send from the desktop mail app just sits in the Outbox and eventually throws a connection-style error. If they use the web mailbox on the browser, sending works. Second: our external project partners are suddenly shown as “Offline” all the time in the chat tool, even while they claim they are online; chats arrive late and calls to them sometimes don’t ring. Third: the multifunction printer says a big scan was “sent successfully”, but when we scan a 20–30 page PDF, nobody receives it (internal or external). Small scans still arrive. Finally: in Basel, the main office printer stopped reacting to print jobs yesterday, and the jobs vanish from the queue as if they never existed, while other printers in the building are fine. People are blaming their laptops, but this looks bigger than that. What is going on? And please confirm, should this be classified as a 1st-level or 2nd-level support ticket?
answer: Recommendation - classification - second_level_support Rationale (evidence from prior cases) - The desktop mail symptom (messages stuck in Outlook Outbox; web/OWA sending OK) matches a documented Exchange/SMTP-auth problem that was escalated and fixed by engineering: in KF22 the root cause was SMTP AUTH being disabled by a tenant Security Default policy; engineers selectively re-enabled SMTP AUTH and adjusted Conditional Access to restore sending [Data: Sources (32)]. - The Scan-to‑Mail symptom (large scans reported "sent" but not delivered) matches a prior KF60 incident where external delivery failed because the printer VLAN/IP range was not allowed by the SMTP relay; resolution required SMTP relay and DNS configuration changes performed by infrastructure teams [Data: Sources (6)]. - The Basel printer symptom (print jobs disappearing from the queue; printer unreachable from the print server) matches a KF60 case where a new printer VLAN had been created without allowing routing from the print server; the fix required a firewall/routing rule change coordinated with the Network Team and updates to meet the Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard [Data: Sources (22)]. - Multiple, simultaneous failures across messaging, presence/chat, and print infrastructure mirror other multi‑system incidents that were escalated beyond first‑line handling (for example, a global Financial Reports outage required system‑engineer level actions and a DataMart reset/escalation to Microsoft) [Data: Sources (0)]. Notes about the chat/presence issue - I did not find a matching prior ticket in the indexed records that documents external chat contacts shown as "Offline" while actually online (presence/calendar/chat latency) - there is no explicit example for that symptom in the provided data. Therefore I cannot point to a documented remediation for this specific symptom from the records. Conclusion and brief justification - Because at least three of the four symptoms map directly to prior incidents that required system‑engineer or network/infrastructure team involvement (Exchange SMTP/Auth, SMTP relay/DNS for scan‑to‑mail, and network/firewall routing for print VLANs), this ticket should be classified as second_level_support. The prior cases show these problems were not resolved by routine first‑line actions alone and required configuration changes and coordination with specialist teams [Data: Sources (32, 6, 22, 0)]. Recommended next steps (these are my recommendations, not items documented in the indexed records) - Recommended: Open a consolidated, second‑level incident and triage per service owner (Exchange/mailflow team, Network/Firewall team, Print/Scanning team, and the chat/application owner) so each symptom can be investigated in parallel. - Recommended: Collect and attach key logs/traces to the incident: Outlook client error codes and event logs, Exchange/SMTP logs and message trace, SMTP relay logs, printer/logs for scan jobs, and network reachability tests from both print server and client subnets. - Recommended: Because previous resolutions required changes to tenant security (SMTP AUTH/Conditional Access), relay allow‑lists, and firewall/routing rules, plan to involve the appropriate specialists early. If you want, I can produce a short incident‑triage checklist (which teams to contact and what logs to request) - note that would be a recommendation and is not taken from the indexed records.

question_text: URGENT SECURITY + ACCESS INCIDENT: Since yesterday afternoon, multiple things broke right after a suspected phishing event. 1) Several employees opened what looked like normal PDF downloads, but then they were redirected to a very convincing Microsoft-style login page. Security now assumes credentials may have been harvested. 2) As an emergency reaction, IT tried to force all non-MFA logins through a country restriction, but now legitimate users traveling are blocked and business-critical work is stopped. 3) At the same time, a few critical automated accounts (backup and an integration service user) started failing to sign in and scheduled jobs broke, but we’re afraid to loosen the security controls. 4) And one internal integration suddenly cannot authenticate anymore even though the same account still works in the browser UI — the automation fails only in the background connection. People are blaming passwords and laptops, but this all started together. What is going on, and please confirm — should this be classified as a 1st-level or 2nd-level support ticket?
answer: Recommendation: second_level_support Summary reasoning (records-based) - The symptom "non-browser/background sign-ins fail while web UI works" maps closely to the TRAIN/DevOps case where non-interactive clients failed due to ADFS client parameters and required system-engineer changes (parameters EnableJWT and RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders were adjusted) - that case was escalated and closed only after engineer intervention [Data: Sources (11)]. - The intermittent SharePoint "access denied" / temporary fixes behaviour matches a SharePoint/AIP case where sensitivity label encryption, unique folder permissions and group membership inconsistencies required in-depth analysis and escalation to the M365/Identity engineering team (System Engineer 11) - the issue could not be resolved by simple Helpdesk steps alone [Data: Sources (30)]. - The desktop mail app sending stuck in Outbox corresponds to a case where SMTP AUTH had been disabled by a tenant security policy (Security Defaults) and a System Engineer identified the root cause (Hybrid Connector / SMTP AUTH) and performed selective re-enabling and Conditional Access adjustments to restore sending [Data: Sources (32)]. - The device-management portal / certificate-bridge showing "unhealthy again" resembles the Domain Controller / Defender sensor tickets where reinstalling/repairing the agent on servers was performed by engineering staff to restore healthy status - again an engineering action rather than a simple first-level fix [Data: Sources (14)]. - Taken together, multiple simultaneous background-signin failures across different subsystems (identity/ADFS, SharePoint/AIP, SMTP/Hybrid, endpoint/agent health) are analogous to prior incidents that required cross-team engineer involvement (including Network/Security or System Engineers) to identify configuration or policy causes and apply targeted fixes [Data: Sources (15), (11), (30), (32), (14)]. What the records explicitly document - DevOps non-browser authentication failures required changing ADFS client parameters and system-engineer activity to resolve (TI-1385257) [Data: Sources (11)]. - Intermittent SharePoint access due to label/permission conflicts required escalation to M365/Identity engineering for in-depth analysis (TI-1578891) [Data: Sources (30)]. - Desktop Outlook unable to send because SMTP AUTH was disabled by Security Defaults; System Engineer adjusted SMTP AUTH/Conditional Access and validated sending (TI-1548823) [Data: Sources (32)]. - Unhealthy sensors / certificate-bridge required agent reinstall / engineer action to restore health (TI-1179834) [Data: Sources (14)]. These items above are factual parallels drawn from the indexed tickets. Recommended next steps (these are my recommendations, not directly documented in the cited records) - Escalate this ticket to second-level (Systems / Identity / Network & Security) and open parallel investigations for each failing subsystem so engineers can correlate logs/policy changes (e.g., tenant security defaults / Conditional Access, ADFS client settings, AIP label encryption behavior, endpoint agent health). - Include timeline of when the problems started and any tenant-wide security or infrastructure changes (patches, policy changes, certificate installs) to help engineers find a common root cause. (Recommendation only - not pulled verbatim from the records.) If you want, I can prepare a concise escalation summary referencing the specific similar tickets (TI-1385257, TI-1578891, TI-1548823, TI-1179834, TI-1594408/TI-1594408-style WAF cases) to attach to the second-level handover.

question_text: Hi Helpdesk… sorry for the chaos but we’re really blocked and it’s all happening at the same time. In SharePoint, the project area for the 2025 migration is basically ‘walled off’ now. The site itself loads, other project spaces open, but THIS one folder/path just throws that classic “you don’t have rights / not allowed” message for a bunch of us who were working in it literally yesterday. Separately (maybe related?), Compliance did some housekeeping: they renamed a folder and slapped one of those ‘Confidential’ / lock-type classifications on it (you know the banner at the top with the label). Since then, access is totally inconsistent. Some people can open files, others get blocked, and one colleague claims it only works if he opens it in a private/incognito browser window. Same user, same link, different result depending on how he opens it… super weird. Also our external auditor can’t deliver their nightly package to our ‘secure drop’ anymore. From their side it just sits there and then times out. On our side it *looks* like the server exists and is up. We even tried WinSCP to reproduce and it behaves the same (hang → fail). They swear they didn’t change anything. While trying to coordinate all this in Teams, one key person’s webcam basically vanished. In Teams settings there’s no camera option at all (not even a dropdown), and even Teams in the browser can’t see a camera. But Windows still lists a camera device in Device Manager, so it doesn’t feel like the hardware is dead. And during the same Teams call, another person can hear everyone perfectly, but nobody can hear them. Teams shows a mic selected, but the little input bar never moves (like it’s stuck at zero). They’re on a USB headset. We already rebooted, swapped USB ports, tried a different browser, and did the usual ‘turn it off and on again’ stuff. Is this connected or just a cursed Monday? And please confirm: should this be classified as a 1st-level or 2nd-level support ticket?
answer: Recommendation (classification) - second_level_support Why (concise reasoning based on prior cases) - The SharePoint symptom you describe-one folder suddenly blocked for some users after a rename and application of a sensitivity/confidential label, with inconsistent visibility (works for some users, works in Incognito for others)-matches a prior incident that required escalation to the M365 / Identity team for in-depth analysis. In that case the label+permission interaction was investigated by a System Engineer and required tests against Azure AD groups and Purview/AIP behavior [Data: Sources (30)]. - A separate but related SharePoint/permission incident (access denied to a single project folder) was resolved only after correcting group nesting in Entra ID (not by simple client-side fixes); the remediation involved changing the Azure AD group structure and synchronizing it, after which access was restored [Data: Sources (20)]. - Your external-auditor upload issue (hang -> fail / WinSCP timeout) resembles past FTP/secure-drop problems that required firewall analysis and coordination with core/network teams and server-side checks rather than purely desktop troubleshooting [Data: Sources (34, 33)]. - Parts of your ticket (Teams camera and mic disappearance) map to cases that were resolved at first-level using standard "Blueprint" checks (OS privacy permissions, app permissions, Teams restart). For example, a missing camera in Teams was fixed by enabling OS camera permissions and app access per the Blueprint [Data: Sources (42)]. However, because multiple distinct subsystems are affected (SharePoint/AIP/Entra ID, secure upload/FTP, and device/media issues in Teams) and SharePoint + upload issues historically required engineer-level changes, the overall ticket should be handled as second_level_support. What the records explicitly document (facts from prior cases) - A sensitivity label enforced encryption and restricted access to an Azure AD group different from the SharePoint permission group; engineers escalated and investigated potential conflicts between SharePoint permissions and AIP encryption [Data: Sources (30)]. - An access-denied case for a SharePoint folder was caused by incorrect group nesting in Entra ID; fixing the nesting and synchronizing restored access [Data: Sources (20)]. - FTP/secure-file delivery problems have previously required firewall and core-team analysis as part of troubleshooting (FileZilla/FTP ticket history) [Data: Sources (34, 33)]. - Teams camera missing from settings was resolved by first-tier steps (OS privacy settings, enabling Teams in allowed apps, Teams restart) following the documented Blueprint [Data: Sources (42)]. Recommended next steps (these are my recommendations and are not verbatim actions from the records) - Escalate the ticket to M365 / Identity (Entra ID) with the SharePoint folder details, the sensitivity label name, and timestamps of when the label was applied. (Recommended; informed by the prior escalation in TI-1578891 [Data: Sources (30)].) - Ask the Identity team to check (a) whether the AIP/sensitivity label enforces encryption to a different Azure AD group than the SharePoint permission group, and (b) group nesting/replication status in Entra ID-both were root causes in similar incidents [Data: Sources (30, 20)]. (Recommended.) - Escalate/coordinate with the Network/Core team for the auditor upload/WinSCP timeouts so they can run firewall/session traces and server-side FTP checks, as similar FTP tickets involved firewall and core-team work [Data: Sources (34, 33)]. (Recommended.) - For the Teams camera/mic issues: have first-level perform the Blueprint checks (OS privacy/app permissions, Teams restart); if the camera/mic do not reappear after those checks, escalate to second-level with remote session logs. The camera case was resolved at first-level via the Blueprint in the record cited [Data: Sources (42)]. (Recommended.) If you want, I can draft an escalation summary you can paste into the second-level ticket (including which logs/screenshots to collect and the tests already done) - that would be a recommended next step not directly pulled from the records.

question_text:
Hi IT-Team, sorry but we have a very odd combination of issues since yesterday and multiple teams are blocked.

First: In D365 (Finance & Operations), Financial Reports is basically unusable. When we go to General Ledger -> Financial Reports -> Edit, it either errors or just spins forever. It is not only one person: several users report it. Also, older reports suddenly show the author as "unknown", and when we try to open or create a report it sometimes says the report cannot be found. This is happening in test and production, so we cannot even use test as a workaround.

Second: our new hotline numbers from the current marketing campaign are dead. Calls to the old main number still ring through, but if a customer calls one of the new ones it fails right away. The carrier insists the calls reach our edge, but they never seem to land with anyone here.

Third: the Basel office has a very specific behavior on outside calls. The call connects, people can talk briefly, and then it cuts off after roughly half a minute almost every time, like clockwork. Internal calls are fine, and colleagues at other sites are fine. Apparently something on our voice setup was changed last week.

Fourth: a few team leads say they no longer get any kind of pop-up or alert when Teams messages come in on their laptops. Messages still arrive — they just only notice them once they actually open Teams. Their phones are unaffected. Windows updates ran last week and at least one person had some focus mode turned on during a meeting.

Is this connected or just multiple incidents at once? And please confirm: should this be classified as a 1st-level or 2nd-level support ticket?
answer: Recommendation I recommend classifying this ticket as second_level_support. Reasoning (mapping each reported symptom to similar documented cases) - D365 Financial Reports unusable (errors, "unknown" authors, "report cannot be found", affects TEST and PROD): This closely matches ticket KF11 / TI-722511 where Financial Reports failed reproducibly across environments and users; the case required Data Mart/FRDB work and a Microsoft support ticket was opened for further analysis [Data: Sources (0, 1)]. - New hotline numbers "dead" (carrier says calls reach your edge but they never land): This matches KF52 / TI-1612036 where incoming calls from a new number range were not delivered because the SBC translation/routing table lacked the new range; resolution required adjusting translation/routing rules per the Direct Routing blueprint (engineering change) [Data: Sources (8)]. - Basel external calls disconnect ~30 seconds after connect (internal calls OK; other sites OK; recent change to voice/VLAN): This mirrors KF52 / TI-1604879 where calls dropped after ~30s due to a 30s NAT/session timeout and session-timer settings; remediation required SBC/session parameter changes and NAT timeout adjustments (coordination with network/voice engineering) [Data: Sources (16)]. - Teams message pop-ups missing on laptops (messages arrive but no pop-up; phones OK; Focus/Windows updates recently): This matches KF52 / TI-1615096 where notifications were blocked by Teams banner settings and Windows Focus Assist; the issue was resolved by standard first-level checks and adjusting notification/Focus Assist settings per the "Blueprint - Teams Notifications Standard" [Data: Sources (19)]. Why second level overall - Three of the four symptoms in this ticket correspond to past incidents that required systems/voice engineering changes or escalation to vendor support (D365 Microsoft ticket; SBC routing/translation changes; NAT/session timer and firewall/NAT timeout adjustments) rather than pure helpdesk-only fixes [Data: Sources (0, 1, 8, 16)]. Only the Teams notification symptom in the bundle maps to a routine first-level fix [Data: Sources (19)]. Given the presence of multiple service domains and multiple items that historically required engineer-level intervention, the overall ticket should be escalated as second_level_support. What the records explicitly document - D365 Financial Reports: reproducible failures across users/environments; attempted Data Mart Reset and FRDB recreation; Microsoft support ticket opened for deeper analysis [Data: Sources (0, 1)]. - Incoming calls from new numbers: SBC lacked translation for the new number range; temporary tests and final adjustment of routing/translation rules per Direct Routing blueprint resolved the issue [Data: Sources (8)]. - 30s call drops (Basel): session timer and NAT/UDP timeout misconfiguration (30s) caused BYE after ~30s; adjusting session timers/NAT timeout on SBC/firewall resolved the problem [Data: Sources (16)]. - Teams notifications: blocked by disabled Teams banner notifications and Windows Focus Assist; standard checks from the Teams Notifications blueprint fixed it [Data: Sources (19)]. Recommended next steps (these are recommendations, not documented steps from the records) - Split the combined report into service-specific tickets (D365, Voice-incoming routing, Voice-Basel session drops, Teams notifications) and assign to the corresponding engineering teams to avoid conflicting changes and to allow parallel work. (recommended) - For D365: escalate to application/system engineers; consider Data Mart/FRDB reset steps and vendor (Microsoft) escalation if initial engineering actions don't restore functionality. (recommended) - For telephony: escalate to Voice/SBC and Network teams to review SBC translation/routing tables (new number ranges), and to verify NAT/session timer and firewall UDP session timeouts for Basel. (recommended) - For Teams notifications: run first-level checks (Teams banner setting, Windows Notification Center/Focus Assist) and resolve locally where applicable. (recommended) If you want, I can draft a short escalation summary (one-paragraph) for your second-level engineers that includes the specific parallels from each cited ticket and the most likely teams to assign.

question_text:
Hi IT, I need help because we have multiple problems at once and people are losing it.

In Zurich (training room), people are trying to make outside calls in Teams. The call goes through and the system shows it's active, the person on the other end can hear us fine, but on our end there's just nothing coming back — total dead air, like our side has no incoming sound at all. We already tried swapping out audio devices entirely (different mic, different speakers) and it makes no difference. Calls between people inside the company work normally.

At the same time in Basel the office is basically unable to print. One new colleague literally has no printers showing up at all on their laptop even though everyone else in the same room can print fine. Another colleague can see the printer in the list, but the moment they try to send something to it they instantly get an "access denied" message and nothing even shows up anywhere. And for the rest of us: documents go into the queue but then just sit there forever like nothing is processing.

We did the usual stuff (restart laptop, unplug dock, change USB port, try a different app) but nothing changed. Does this look connected or just bad timing, and please confirm: should this be classified as a 1st-level or 2nd-level support ticket?
answer: Summary recommendation - Classification: second_level_support. What the records explicitly document (evidence) - External Teams calls with one‑way audio (internal OK, external silent) in Zurich were previously diagnosed as a media/firewall/NAT configuration issue affecting media ports or session timers. Resolution required Unified Communications/SBC and network adjustments according to the Direct Routing architecture (changes made by engineers; tests with SBC/session parameters; firewall/NAT timeouts adjusted). This is documented in tickets TI-1602241 and TI-1604879 [Data: Sources (5, 16, 23)]. - Multiple types of printing failures (printer not responding after printers were moved into a new VLAN; single user "Access denied" from an explicit deny on the printer queue; default printer switching because of AD/GPO group membership; SAP printing broken after VM restore causing print‑share issues) were diagnosed and resolved by system engineers or required coordination with the Network/Core teams. Remediations included adjusting routing/firewall rules between server and printer VLAN, removing explicit deny permissions on the print queue, cleaning up AD group memberships or re‑enabling network shares on print servers - all actions done per the Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard or by engineering staff [Data: Sources (22, 3, 2, 28)]. Why this ticket should be second_level_support - The Zurich Teams symptom matches prior cases that were not solvable by client‑side steps (reinstalling, changing headsets, rebooting) and required SBC/network session and firewall changes performed by specialists (Direct Routing / NAT / session timer work) [Data: Sources (5, 16, 23)]. - The Basel printing symptoms combine multiple failure modes seen before that required server/queue permission changes, AD/GPO corrections, or network/VLAN routing and firewall rule changes - each of which in the past involved system engineers and coordination with network/core teams, not just front‑line user fixes [Data: Sources (22, 3, 2, 28)]. Recommended operational approach (this is my recommendation, not extracted from the records) - Treat this as a second‑level incident and escalate to Unified Communications (for the Teams one‑way audio) and to Print/Core + Network teams (for the multiple printing problems). Consider opening separate escalations for the Teams issue and the printing issues to allow parallel engineer workflows. (Recommended) If you want, I can draft an escalation summary you can paste into your ticket system that references the symptoms and asks for UC/SBC and Print/Core+Network involvement (marked as recommended).

question_text:
Hi IT, we need help asap because it feels like the whole company is falling apart since the weekend changes. I'm trying to describe it without guessing, but it's a mess.

1) In D365 (F&O) the Financial Reports / Report Designer thing is basically broken. It either loads forever or errors out. What's super strange: older reports suddenly show the author as 'unknown' and sometimes it says a report 'cannot be found' even when we used it last week. This happens in both test and prod, so we can't even use test as a workaround.

2) Sales: the desktop mail app is acting up. People can receive emails, but anything they send just sits in Outbox and later fails with a connection-style error. If they send the same email from the browser mailbox it goes through. Mobile seems fine too.

3) External partners: in Teams they are shown as Offline all day even while they are actively writing to us. Chats arrive late and sometimes calls to them do not ring. Internals are fine.

4) Zurich site: external calls in Teams connect, the other side says they hear us, but on our side it's dead silent (zero audio). We swapped headsets and speakers and it's the same. Internal calls are ok.

5) Basel site: external calls connect and then drop after about half a minute almost every time, like a timer. Internal calls are ok.

6) Also with the new marketing hotline numbers: calls to the old main number work, but calls to the new number range fail instantly. The provider claims they deliver the call to our edge.

7) Printing is chaos too, but it's not one single thing. A new employee in Basel sees literally no printers at all, even though everyone next to them can print. Another user can see the printer but gets 'Access denied' immediately when printing. And for the rest of us: jobs show up in the queue and then just sit there forever like nothing is processing.

8) One more: in Basel the main office printer sometimes shows jobs for a second and then they vanish, while other printers in the building still work.

People keep blaming laptops, docks, and Windows updates, but all of this started at basically the same time after the weekend work. Is this one connected change or just unlucky timing? And please confirm: should this be classified as a 1st-level or 2nd-level support ticket?
answer: Recommendation: second_level_support What the records explicitly document that supports this classification - The D365 Financial Reports / Report Designer failure you describe (reports loading forever, "unknown" authors, "report cannot be found", affecting TEST and PROD) closely matches the KF11 incident where Financial Reports were broken across environments; that case required a Data Mart reset and a Microsoft support ticket was opened - i.e. it was not solved by simple first‑line steps [Data: Sources (0, 1)]. - The symptom "external Teams calls drop after ~30 seconds" maps directly to a past incident where calls at one site disconnected after ~30s due to session timers and NAT/UDP timeouts on the voice VLAN; resolution required SBC/session parameter changes and coordination with the network team to adjust NAT timeouts per the Direct Routing architecture [Data: Sources (16)]. - The printing problems you describe (users seeing no printers, "Access denied", jobs stuck/disappearing, site‑specific behavior) match multiple prior cases that were resolved only after engineer actions: e.g. a print server network‑share problem after a Windows update/VM restore that required re‑enabling network shares on the print server and engineer intervention [Data: Sources (28)], and a site where printers were put into a new VLAN but routing/firewall rules were missing so the print server couldn't reach printers - the network/firewall rules had to be corrected by the network team [Data: Sources (22)]. - Authentication/ADFS style issues that needed parameter changes (EnableJWT / RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders) were treated as second‑level work and required specific ADFS client parameter adjustments in a prior case [Data: Sources (11)]. Why this points to second_level_support - Your ticket describes simultaneous failures across multiple platforms (D365/F&O, SMTP/Outlook client vs webmail, Teams presence/voice, telephony provider handoffs, and printing across VLANs). The indexed incidents show that each of these types of failures was previously resolved only after engineer/architect involvement (Data Mart reset + Microsoft support for D365; SBC/NAT/session timer and firewall/NAT adjustments for Teams/Direct Routing; print‑server share and VLAN routing fixes for printing). Because the current problems span multiple services and require coordination between application, network, telephony and print engineers, it aligns with previously escalated, second‑level incidents [Data: Sources (0, 1, 16, 28, 22)]. Notes about items not directly matched in the records - The Outlook/desktop mail client behavior (send stuck in Outbox but webmail works) is not directly documented in the indexed tickets I can cite. I cannot state from the records what the root cause of that specific symptom was in other cases because an exact match is not present in the indexed data. - The provider claim that calls are delivered to your edge and the failing new hotline number are telephony/edge configuration issues; the records show similar provider/edge interplay required network/SBC adjustments for Teams/Direct Routing, but there is no single record that documents an identical "new number range fails while old works" scenario. Recommended next actions (these are my recommendations, not steps documented in the cited records) - Escalate to second‑level/network/telephony engineers immediately and open one consolidated incident for cross‑team coordination (Apps/D365, Exchange/Email, Network/SBC, Telephony Provider, Print Services). The breadth and cross‑service nature argue for a coordinated second‑level response. - Provide logs/traces for D365 Report Designer, Exchange/Outlook SMTP/MAPI logs for failed sends, SBC/Direct Routing logs for the Teams calls, and recent network/VLAN/firewall change records for Basel/Zurich (to help correlate a single change across services). If you want, I can map each of your numbered symptoms to the most relevant prior ticket(s) in the index and produce a short summary per symptom citing the exact source ids.
